steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has a long noun clause as its subject: 'That the repeated thermonuclear outbursts of U Scorpii... have occurred at intervals as short as eight years — shorter intervals between outbursts than have been observed in some other novae of its class —'. A noun clause subject is grammatically singular. The blank must supply the main verb of the sentence so it forms a complete independent clause.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘is due’: A finite, singular verb that matches the singular noun-clause subject. The sentence becomes a complete clause: the subject ‘That the outbursts...’ IS DUE to the white dwarf’s mass. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘due’: A bare adjective or preposition with no verb. Without a finite verb the sentence has no main predicate and is a fragment. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘being due’: A non-finite participle. It cannot serve as a main verb, so no independent clause is formed. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘having been due’: Another non-finite form (perfect participle). Like C, it cannot anchor a main clause. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
